frameworks.name: neural correlates of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: search for brain signals specific to awareness independent of attention

frameworks.name: attention-matching paradigm
frameworks.one_line: equating attention across awareness conditions to isolate awareness-specific activity

frameworks.name: attention vs awareness dissociation
frameworks.one_line: treating attention and awareness as separable processes requiring careful experimental disentangling

core_question: Can the proposed attention-matching method successfully disentangle attention from awareness in fMRI studies of consciousness?
key_assumptions: attention and awareness are dissociable processes
behavioral attention indices can index attentional state across awareness conditions
matching attention across conditions isolates awareness-specific neural signals
manipulations: critique of an existing fMRI experimental design
key_findings: the targeted experimental design conflicts with known features of attention
logical and methodological gaps remain in disentangling attention from awareness
interpretations: attention-matching as proposed does not cleanly isolate neural correlates of awareness
rigorous attention-awareness dissociation requires additional methodological controls
